dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 5 for an overdose/poisoning ingestion call at Richmond.
Hello, my name is Josh, an EMT with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
I took some meds. I was trying to end it. It started this afternoon.
Can you tell me your name and the date today?
Jake, 41. Today is the 18th.
What county are we in?
Richmond county.
What happened today?
I tried to kill myself with some stuff I had at home.
Sir, if it's okay, we want to do a few checks to make sure you're safe. With that okay, can we continue?
Go ahead.
You're breathing, your airway is open.
I'm going to check your breathing. Can you raise your arms for me? Deep breath in.
Nice deep breath out.
Respirations are 20, normal in rate and depth.
SpO2 is at 96, good.
Can we check your pulse now?
Pulse is 93, strong and regular.
Pulse is 93, strong and regular.
Blood pressure is 131 over 85.
Blood sugar is 132.
EKG shows sinus rhythm.
What symptoms are you experiencing the most now?
Abdominal pain, nausea, and I feel light-headed.
What medications do you usually take?
Lisinopril, metformin, Depakote, and Geodon.
Do you have any allergies?
Penicillin and Amoxicillin.
What about your medical history-diagnosed with any conditions?
I'm diabetic, on insulin, have hypertension, had an old MI, and also BPH.
Any other conditions or surgeries?
I have anxiety, bipolar, and epilepsy.
What did you take today or in the last 24 hours?
I took six 800mg ibuprofen, ten prednisone, Depakote, doxycycline… and some other drugs.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: When did you take these medications?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Between 2 PM and 6 PM.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What was going on in your mind before you took them?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I've been hearing voices telling me to die. I tried to get help last night but was sent home.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What other substances have you used today?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I've had alcohol, marijuana, meth, cocaine… and heroin today.
When did your symptoms begin today?
Right after I took the medicines this afternoon.
What makes the pain worsen?
It's worse when I move or breathe deeply. I also feel dizzy.
Can you describe the pain to me?
It's a heavy pressure in my stomach.
Does the pain spread to anywhere else?
No, it's only in the abdomen.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is your pain right now?
It's a 9, really intense.
We're going to place you for an intravenous start now.
Sir, we are going to insert an IV in your arm. Is that okay?
Yeah, go ahead.
IV placed successfully with 18g needle in the left arm.
Your blood glucose is 132, normal at the time of setup.
We'll now hook you up to an EKG for heart monitoring.
EKG monitoring in place-4 lead monitoring.
Your skin is warm, dry, and looks normal in color.
Eyes steady and reactive. You seem alert and oriented.
Your breathing seems normal with no funny sounds in your lungs.
I'm going to do a quick exam of your abdomen. Can we proceed?
Abdomen is soft with no distension noted, but tender to palpation in the central region.
I understand you use a different method for taking drugs. Can we talk about that?
Yeah, I shoot it through there, it's the easiest for me.
We need to check you for stroke symptoms. Can you raise both arms for me and hold them for a second?
And smile for me, please. Looks good-no signs of stroke.
Your blood glucose is 0 now. That concerns us. Please stay with us.
Pulse is 96 now, still in regular rhythm.
Respirations are down to 14, still shallow but consistent with normal.
Blood pressure is now 122 over 81, still within a normal range.
Oxygen saturation remains at 97-no change so far.
EKG rhythm is still sinus-no change. No acute issues in rhythm observed at this time.
Tympanic temperature is 99.1°F. Slightly elevated, but not concerning for now.
We'll keep watching your blood glucose-your second check shows concern, but so far no intervention is needed.
GCS is still 15, meaning you're alert, oriented and no neurological concerns at this moment.
Extraocular movements remain smooth and normal-no signs of acute confusion or loss.
We're doing a 12-lead EKG now for a full cardiac evaluation to cross-check findings.
12-lead EKG performed and interpreted as normal sinus rhythm with no acute cardiac issues.
A 10 mL saline flush has been done on your IV to allow easy access if needed during transport.
We're following the non-emergent overdose/poisoning protocol-Protocol 7-1.
How is your pain now?
Pain level is still 9 and constant. It hasn't changed any this far.
Pulse is 96 and breathing at 14. No big deviation from before.
BP remains at 122 over 81, O2 at 97. Still stable here.
Where do you want to go for treatment? We have orders for VCU.
VCU works for me-right across from where I was at a different ED last night.
Orders confirm we are taking you to VCU Health System per dispatch instructions.
We'll transfer you to the stretcher now for a safe journey as standard protocol for transport safety.
5-point harness installed and all straps secured to prevent any movement issues during the ride.
We'll have your medications securely stored with your belongings and hand them off with your care as per protocol.
Did you attempt to remove your IV while in transport? We need that info for documentation.
Yeah, I wanted to stop the process once things got worse. I was in a lot of pain.
For safety, you were placed in soft restraints briefly to calm the situation and prevent injury.
Hospital staff were on standby with instructions on our decision for restraint use at VCU ED upon arrival.
All your meds were already handed to the ED staff along with your EKG and IV setup records.
We are taking you to bed 9. VCU was delayed, but we secured a spot and staff are ready for you.
Final vitals before arrival show consistent readings. No sign of new issues.
A full report has been sent to VCU, including patient history, overdose event details, and transfer plans.
Criteria for protocol 7-1 were all confirmed and documented throughout including the IV, EKG, and blood glucose readings.
You are safe and secured, and we can begin transporting now. Emergency medical care is complete per protocol.
